docs.title: Getting Started
docs.description: What SnowGlobe is, who uses it, and how Snow Angels should navigate the internal HQ.

sections.heading: What SnowGlobe is
sections.body: SnowGlobe is the private internal HQ for Snow Digital Group Snow Angels: a calm operating manual for people, protocols, playbooks, and protected standards.

sections.heading: Who uses it
sections.body: SnowGlobe is for approved Snow Digital Group employees, trusted operators, helpers, caregivers, and collaborators. Access is controlled outside this static app.

sections.heading: What belongs here
sections.body: Internal standards, operating playbooks, handoff formats, escalation rules, changelog notes, and resource indexes belong here.

sections.heading: What does not belong here
sections.body: Do not store secrets, real private family data, medical records, addresses, phone numbers, schedules, customer data, or credentials in this V1 static portal.

docs.title: Mission
docs.description: The operating purpose behind SnowGlobe and the Snow Angels culture.

sections.heading: Snow Digital Group
sections.body: Snow Digital Group exists to build calm, intelligent, beautiful growth systems for clients and internal teams.

sections.heading: SnowGlobe
sections.body: SnowGlobe keeps the people behind those systems aligned, protected, and operational. It is the internal memory layer for standards, handoffs, and rhythm.

sections.heading: Operating promise
sections.body: Every Snow Angel should know what matters, where to find it, when to escalate, and how to leave the system cleaner than they found it.

docs.title: Snow Angels
docs.description: Definition and expectations for approved internal collaborators.

sections.heading: Definition
sections.body: Snow Angels are trusted Snow Digital Group employees, operators, approved helpers, and care collaborators who uphold the company's care, quality, and trust standards.

sections.heading: What the role means
sections.body: The role is serious: protect confidentiality, communicate clearly, document cleanly, and escalate early when the situation needs authority.

sections.heading: What the role is not
sections.body: Snow Angels do not improvise on safety matters, make unauthorized care decisions, speak publicly for Snow Digital Group, or access information outside their approved scope.

docs.title: Standards
docs.description: The baseline behavior contract for Snow Angels.

sections.heading: Core standards
sections.body: Snow Angels work with calm authority, clean handoffs, confidentiality, punctuality, and documentation discipline.

sections.heading: Handoff discipline
sections.body: A good handoff makes the next person more capable. It records what happened, what changed, what is pending, and what needs attention.

sections.heading: Escalation norm
sections.body: Early escalation is a strength. If a situation is unclear, safety-related, emotionally heated, client-sensitive, or outside scope, raise it.

docs.title: Client Ops
docs.description: Principles for communication, ownership, delivery quality, and escalation.

sections.heading: Communication principles
sections.body: Client communication should be clear, warm, scoped, and accountable. Avoid overpromising. Confirm next steps in writing.

sections.heading: Task ownership
sections.body: Every task needs an owner, a desired output, a delivery moment, and an escalation path if blocked.

sections.heading: Delivery quality
sections.body: Delivery should feel polished: accurate files, clean naming, short summaries, and clear notes about assumptions or unresolved questions.

docs.title: Care Ops
docs.description: Non-medical care operations rules for approved caregivers and helpers.
docs.caution: Care Ops is operational, not medical advice. Follow parent or guardian instructions and escalate safety concerns immediately.

sections.heading: Approved caregivers only
sections.body: Only approved caregivers and helpers may participate in care operations. No unauthorized visitors, substitutions, or unscheduled handoffs.

sections.heading: Parent instructions control
sections.body: Follow parent or guardian instructions. Do not make medication, food, allergy, transport, visitor, or schedule decisions without parent or guardian approval.

sections.heading: Future protected notes
sections.body: Food, allergy, schedule, and detailed care notes belong in a future protected care module, not in this static V1 content.

docs.title: Emergency
docs.description: Immediate escalation rules for urgent situations.
docs.caution: Do not delay emergency care to update SnowGlobe.

sections.heading: Immediate danger
sections.body: Call 911 first for immediate danger, breathing difficulty, serious injury, fire, suspected life-threatening emergency, or any urgent situation where waiting would be unsafe.

sections.heading: Parent or guardian notification
sections.body: After emergency services are contacted and it is safe to do so, contact the parent or guardian. Do not delay urgent action to send updates.

sections.heading: Possible poisoning
sections.body: For possible poisoning or exposure, use Poison Control guidance and contact the parent or guardian. In the United States, Poison Control is available at 1-800-222-1222.

sections.heading: Incident note
sections.body: After urgent action is complete, document what happened, who was present, what action was taken, who was contacted, and what follow-up is needed.

docs.title: Escalation
docs.description: When and how Snow Angels should raise issues.

sections.heading: Escalate early
sections.body: Escalate when a decision touches safety, privacy, client trust, care instructions, money, public communication, or anything outside the approved scope.

sections.heading: Escalation format
sections.body: Use a concise format: situation, risk, current action, decision needed, and deadline.

docs.title: Incident Notes
docs.description: How to document incidents after urgent action is complete.

sections.heading: Sequence matters
sections.body: Handle immediate safety first. Then document the incident. SnowGlobe notes must never slow down emergency action.

sections.heading: What to capture
sections.body: Record time, location category, people present, what happened, immediate action, parent or guardian notification, emergency services if used, and follow-up needed.

docs.title: Handoff
docs.description: The minimum useful handoff format for care, client, and operations work.

sections.heading: Minimum handoff
sections.body: A handoff should make the next person confident quickly.

sections.heading: Care-specific additions
sections.body: When relevant and parent-approved, include meals or bottles, nap or sleep notes, mood or behavior observations, and any parent or guardian follow-up.

docs.title: Docs Changelog
docs.description: How SnowGlobe docs changes are versioned and communicated.

sections.heading: Versioning
sections.body: SnowGlobe docs use simple semantic versions for major internal changes, new sections, and launch-ready releases.

sections.heading: Change discipline
sections.body: Every meaningful update should explain what changed, why it matters, and whether Snow Angels need to adjust behavior.

playbooks.title: Client Delivery
playbooks.description: A practical delivery rhythm for small Snow Digital projects.
steps: Name the owner and due date.
Do the work in the cleanest available format.
Deliver with a short summary and next step.

playbooks.title: House Standards
playbooks.description: Calm, clean, respectful standards for shared spaces and trusted helpers.
steps: Escalate safety or access issues immediately.

playbooks.title: Snow Care
playbooks.description: A non-medical care operations starter for approved caregivers only.

playbooks.title: Communications
playbooks.description: How Snow Angels communicate with clarity and calm.

resources.title: Brand Assets
resources.description: Index for approved logos, visual assets, and templates once protected asset storage is ready.

resources.title: Templates
resources.description: Reusable handoff, client update, incident note, and checklist templates.

resources.title: Checklists
resources.description: Simple operational checklists for Snow Angels.
